name: Speedy Cash
description_short: Speedy Cash offers payday loans up to $425, lines of credit up to $4,000, and title loans up to $4,000 with in-store and online applications at Memphis locations.
description_long: Speedy Cash is a storefront lender operating multiple locations in the Memphis, Tennessee area, including the Summer Avenue location profiled here. The company operates under the tagline "Tomorrow can be payday®" and positions itself as a neighborhood financial services provider for consumers needing short-term cash solutions. The company has been serving the Memphis market with physical retail locations that combine lending services with ancillary financial services. Speedy Cash offers three primary loan products: Payday Loans (up to $425 with repayment due on the next payday), Lines of Credit (up to $4,000 with flexible draw and repayment terms), and Title Loans (up to $4,000 using a vehicle as collateral). All three products are available for application both in-store and online. The company emphasizes transparency, requiring applicants to understand terms and costs before signing, and offers personalized guidance through their in-store teams. What distinguishes Speedy Cash is its hybrid retail-and-digital approach, allowing customers to choose between in-person service with immediate questions answered or online application convenience. The Summer Avenue store maintains extended hours (Monday-Friday with evening hours on Monday and Friday, Saturday mornings, closed Sundays), accommodating working customers. Beyond lending, Speedy Cash provides ancillary money services including check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, and Green Dot debit card services. The company operates a multi-store network across the Memphis area with locations on Getwell Road, Austin Peay Highway, Lamar Avenue, and other major thoroughfares, plus nearby Bartlett locations. Speedy Cash clearly targets consumers in urgent financial situations who need access to small-to-moderate amounts of cash quickly. However, the company operates in the high-cost short-term lending space, and while it provides transparent terms, the actual APR and total cost of borrowing are not disclosed on this location page, which is typical for payday and title lenders. The products themselves—payday loans and title loans—carry inherent risks including debt cycles, vehicle loss potential, and substantial interest costs that regulators and consumer advocates have flagged as problematic for vulnerable populations.
pros: Multiple loan products available (payday, line of credit, title loans) to match different borrowing needs
Extended hours including evenings (until 7pm Monday and Friday) for working customers
Can apply online or in-person, with in-person option providing real-time clarification of terms
Clear documentation requirements listed upfront to ensure efficient application process
Multiple Memphis-area locations for convenience (at least 8 locations mentioned)
Additional money services available in-store (check cashing, wire transfers, money orders, debit cards)
Transparency requirement that borrowers understand exact costs before signing loan documents
cons: No APR or total cost of borrowing information disclosed on the location page, making true cost comparison impossible
Title loans require clear title or first lien, limiting access for some borrowers and creating vehicle repossession risk
Payday loans cap at $425, which may be insufficient for many emergency situations
No disclosure of actual approval rates, default rates, or what happens if customers cannot repay on schedule
Line of Credit up to $4,000 still represents relatively small amounts for extended financial needs
best_for: Employed individuals needing small cash amounts ($100-$425) by their next paycheck with transparent terms
Vehicle owners facing unexpected expenses who want to leverage title collateral for larger loans ($1,000-$4,000)
Customers in the Memphis area who prefer in-person financial interactions and immediate answers to questions
Those needing variable access to cash through a line of credit rather than a lump sum
diagnosis: Speedy Cash is best for employed Memphis-area residents facing genuine short-term cash emergencies who understand and accept the high costs of payday/title lending. The main caveat is that these are inherently expensive borrowing products (APR not disclosed) that can trap borrowers in debt cycles, and title loans specifically carry the risk of vehicle loss—this is appropriate only for borrowers with no other options and clear repayment plans.
services: Green Dot Visa Debit Card services
